fieldResolution.detail: Field resolution is graded by the base harness, not the critic slice.

reasoning.detail: The critic fired 11 real bedrock calls, one per candidate finding.

outcomes.label: A · pseudoreplication (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 2.35e-7 collapses to a non-significant honestP of 0.775 when properly aggregated to the 4 donor-level replicates, showing the apparent effect was driven by treating thousands of cells as independent when they share donor-level variation (ICC=0.52 indicates substantial within-donor correlation).

outcomes.label: A · double dipping (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The held-out AUC dropped from 0.61 to 0.58, approaching chance, and zero of four markers retained discriminative power on the independent split, with 560 held-out cells providing adequate power to detect real separation.

outcomes.label: A · fragility, Effector (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The Effector cluster never formed as a discrete cluster in any of the 10 resolution settings tested, indicating it is not a reproducible cell state but rather an artifact of a specific resolution choice outside the sweep range.

outcomes.label: A · confounding (genuine)
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1 indicates perfect collinearity between condition and the technical variable lane, and the rank-deficient design confirms the biological and technical effects cannot be separated, so the confounding flag is warranted.

outcomes.label: B · pseudoreplication (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 1.3e-06 collapses to an honest replicate-level p-value of 0.72, far above the 0.05 threshold, with only 6 patients as the true independent units and a high ICC of 0.61 indicating substantial within-patient correlation that inflated the naive test.

outcomes.label: B · confounding (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1 indicates perfect collinearity between the grouping variable and the technical nuisance variable, and the rank-deficient design matrix confirms these effects cannot be separated statistically.

outcomes.label: C · pseudoreplication (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The honest replicate-level p-value is 4.8e-06, far below the 0.05 threshold, so the effect survives aggregation to the donor level and this is not pseudoreplication.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The held-out AUC is 1.0, identical to the discovery AUC, with all 4 markers holding on 570 held-out cells, so the markers fully replicate out of sample and the flag is unwarranted.

outcomes.label: C · fragility (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The Rare state formed as a discrete cluster in all 10 of 10 resolution settings tested, giving perfect stability of 1.0, which means the cluster is robust across the entire resolution sweep and not a resolution artifact.

outcomes.label: C · confounding (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 0.033 indicates near-zero association between the grouping variable and technical factors, the design matrix is full rank, and the variables are explicitly marked separable, so there is no confounding to flag.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (underpowered split)
outcomes.justification: The held-out split contains only 14 cells, which is too few to reliably estimate AUC for a 2-marker signature; the drop from 0.74 to 0.59 could easily reflect sampling noise rather than true separation loss.

selfTests.name: rubber-stamp critic is caught
selfTests.detail: A rubber-stamp critic failed to veto the over-fires and downgrade the underpowered split, so the harness rejects it.

selfTests.name: a failed critic call fails safe toward showing
selfTests.detail: When the critic call throws, genuine findings stay flagged (shown by default), never hidden.
